friction_items.description: qualitative_pii_redaction step `detect_and_redact` instructs the researcher to invoke `tool_python_exec script='scripts/qual_pii_redact.py' ...` but no such script ships with the project. The protocol punts the actual presidio + spaCy + regex pipeline to a script the researcher must author from scratch. A natural-language researcher will not know how to compose a presidio analyzer with a deterministic pseudonym map, will hit env errors (en_core_web_lg not installed), and will give up.
friction_items.suggested_fix: Ship `templates/scripts/qual_pii_redact.py` (or a first-class `tool_qualitative_pii_redact` MCP tool) that does the work end-to-end. The protocol then becomes 'call tool_qualitative_pii_redact' rather than 'write a presidio pipeline.'

friction_items.description: qualitative_pii_redaction BLOCKs if the policy doesn't explicitly address all 18 HIPAA Safe Harbor classes — including biometric identifiers, vehicle plates, device serials, MRN — for an 8-participant psychology interview study where 12 of the 18 are obviously inapplicable. Researcher is forced to type `NOT_APPLICABLE` 12 times just to unblock.
friction_items.suggested_fix: Default the policy template to NOT_APPLICABLE for the 18 classes, then require the researcher to confirm only the ones that ARE applicable. Inverts the cost: address what matters, accept defaults for what doesn't.

friction_items.description: The `create_step` step asks the researcher to hand-write `step_summary.yaml` with fields like `step_intent: ground`, `figure_required: false`, `table_required: false`. The researcher has no idea this YAML governs downstream completeness audits. They will skip it, then later hit a BLOCK on 'missing required figure' for a study that intentionally has no figures.
friction_items.suggested_fix: Auto-generate step_summary.yaml from the protocol's `expected_outputs` / `quality_bar` block in the loader. Researcher never touches it. If they want to override, expose `sys_step_intent_set` as a single-line tool.

friction_items.description: For an 8-interview study the researcher is on the hook for: pii_policy.md, pseudonym_map.csv, raw_data_redacted/, redaction_ledger_*.csv, redaction_audit.md, interview_protocol.md, codebook.md, themes.md, trustworthiness.md, reflexivity.md, intercoder_agreement.md, member_checking.md, quote_anonymisation_audit.md, saturation_curve.png, saturation_check.md, qualitative_quality_audit.md, conclusions.md, plus optional thematic-map figure. ~18 mandatory artefacts for N=8 is heavy enough that a pragmatic researcher will skip the audit pass entirely.
friction_items.suggested_fix: Introduce a `pilot` or `small-N` mode in researcher_config that collapses intercoder + member_checking + saturation into a single `small_n_limitations.md` with one paragraph each. Reserve the full COREQ/SRQR battery for N >= 15 or `mode: publication`.

friction_items.description: qualitative_quality_audit step `intercoder_agreement_check` accepts single-coder analysis as a documented limitation — but the gate language ('A verdict < 0.70 without a reconciliation plan is a BLOCKER') is easy to mis-read. The first time a researcher with one coder hits this, they will think they're blocked from synthesis and will burn an LLM turn arguing with the protocol.
friction_items.suggested_fix: Split the gate: if `coders == 1` AND `limitation_documented`, auto-write the limitation row and PASS. Only BLOCK when coders >= 2 AND kappa < 0.70 AND no reconciliation. Make the branch explicit in the YAML, not folded into prose.

friction_items.description: 204 distinct tools (sys_/tool_/mem_) surface at startup. For this scenario the researcher needs ~15 (sys_path, sys_file_read/write, sys_protocol_get, sys_protocol_log, tool_python_exec, tool_qualitative_*, tool_audit_synthesis, mem_log). The other 189 are noise that bloats AI context and makes `tool_protocols_list` / `sys_active_tools` unwieldy. Routing exists but the user still pays the registration cost on every session.
friction_items.suggested_fix: Phase 9 consolidation: gate tool registration behind active protocol / active pack. A researcher who hasn't routed to slurm / cytoscape / nextflow shouldn't see those tools. Default-active set should be < 50 tools.

friction_items.description: tool_qualitative_quote_provenance requires one tool call per quote, with the researcher supplying quote text, participant_id, transcript_path, and line_range. A typical 5-theme paper with 2-3 quotes per theme = 15+ tool calls just to register provenance. The researcher will skip it and then fail the qualitative_report_format audit that consumes the registry.
friction_items.suggested_fix: Add `tool_qualitative_quotes_bulk_register` that takes a CSV / JSON file and registers in one call. Or scrape themes.md for `[P01:42-47]`-style markers and auto-populate the registry.

friction_items.description: tool_qualitative_select_standard auto-mode reads workspace/study_design.yaml, but no protocol creates this file. The 'auto' default silently falls back to SRQR with a confused reason string ('study_design.yaml not found; defaulting to SRQR'). The researcher gets the wrong standard chosen and doesn't know why.
friction_items.suggested_fix: qualitative_research step `design_protocol` should also emit workspace/study_design.yaml (a minimal {method, population, n_target} stub). Or have select_standard fall through to interview_protocol.md when study_design.yaml is missing.

friction_items.description: qualitative_pii_redaction description leads with '45 CFR §164.514(b)(2)', 'GDPR Article 4(5)', 'presidio', 'spaCy en_core_web_lg', 'Microsoft Presidio'. A domain researcher (anthropology, education, nursing) without infra background will bounce. The protocol is technically correct but the on-ramp is regulatory.
friction_items.suggested_fix: Lead the description with a one-sentence plain-language summary ('Replaces names + employers + locations + dates in transcripts with stable pseudonyms before coding starts'), then drop the regulatory citations into a `compliance:` block lower down.

friction_items.description: Five qualitative protocols (qualitative_research, qualitative_pii_redaction, qualitative_quality_audit, thematic_analysis_braun_clarke, grounded_theory_iteration, coding_scheme_iteration, member_checking, qualitative_report_format) overlap on triggers like 'qualitative', 'coding', 'codebook'. tool_route will pick one but the researcher doesn't know the difference between qualitative_research and thematic_analysis_braun_clarke. The 'pack' protocol vs 'core' protocol distinction is invisible.
friction_items.suggested_fix: Flatten: have qualitative_research be the umbrella that hands off to the method-specific pack protocol (thematic vs grounded vs framework) at a single decision point. Or surface pack provenance in the router's choice message ('Chose pack:qualitative/thematic_analysis_braun_clarke because you mentioned Braun & Clarke').

friction_items.description: qualitative_quality_audit's saturation_evidence_check expects a cumulative-unique-codes curve and BLOCKs with 'NOT SATURATED' if the slope at the last transcript is > 0. With N=8, saturation curves are noisy and the protocol's binary verdict is statistically dubious. Researcher gets a hard verdict on something that's genuinely uncertain.
friction_items.suggested_fix: Make the verdict three-valued (SATURATED | LIKELY_NOT_SATURATED | UNDERPOWERED_TO_JUDGE) and emit UNDERPOWERED_TO_JUDGE when N < 12. Pair with an explicit recommendation rather than a BLOCK.

friction_items.description: tool_audit_synthesis returns failure reasons that reference internal field names ('step_summary.figure_required mismatch', 'expected_outputs not satisfied') rather than what the researcher should do. A natural-language researcher will paste the error back to the LLM and hope for the best.
friction_items.suggested_fix: Audit failures should be (a) one human sentence ('Your themes.md cites P03 but the quote registry has no P03 entry') (b) the suggested fix ('Run tool_qualitative_quote_provenance for the quote on themes.md:42') and only then (c) the structured field name for the AI.

friction_items.description: tool_qualitative_codebook_diff hardcodes the output filename to 'diff_v1_to_v2.md' regardless of the input version numbers passed in. Diffing v3 -> v4 overwrites the v1 -> v2 file silently.
friction_items.suggested_fix: Parse the version numbers from the input filenames (or accept explicit v1_label/v2_label args) and name the output accordingly.

friction_items.description: The three qualitative pack tools (codebook_diff, quote_provenance, select_standard) are defined in src/research_os_qualitative/tools.py via register_tool but do not appear in server.py's TOOL_DEFINITIONS dump. A grep-driven researcher won't find them; only the runtime plugin registry surfaces them. Discovery depends on tool_protocols_list which not all researchers know to call.
friction_items.suggested_fix: Surface pack-contributed tools in sys_help / sys_tool_describe with a 'pack: qualitative' tag. Add an example in the qualitative protocol descriptions ('Use tool_qualitative_codebook_diff to compare codebook versions').

recommendations: Ship a first-class tool_qualitative_pii_redact MCP tool that wraps the presidio + spaCy + regex pipeline end-to-end. Remove the 'write your own scripts/qual_pii_redact.py' burden from the protocol.

Add a `study_size: pilot | standard | publication` knob to researcher_config that collapses the COREQ/SRQR battery for N < 12. Pilot mode auto-documents intercoder, saturation, and member-checking absences as accepted limitations.
Auto-generate workspace/<step>/step_summary.yaml from the protocol's expected_outputs + quality_bar at sys_path time. Eliminate the hand-written contract step entirely.
Invert the PII policy default: 18 HIPAA classes default to NOT_APPLICABLE; researcher confirms only the ones that apply. Drops a HIGH-friction gate to LOW.

Phase 9: gate tool registration on active protocol/pack. Default-active surface < 50 tools. Lazy-load slurm / cytoscape / nextflow / latex / dashboard tools only after the router picks a protocol that needs them.
Add tool_qualitative_quotes_bulk_register that takes a CSV/JSON file and registers provenance in one call. Avoid the 15+ tool-call-per-paper overhead.
Lead protocol descriptions with one plain-language sentence; move regulatory citations and library names into a `compliance:` / `dependencies:` block lower down.
Rewrite audit failure messages: (1) human sentence, (2) suggested fix tool call, (3) structured field name. Stop surfacing 'step_summary.figure_required mismatch' as the headline.
Surface pack-contributed tools (tool_qualitative_codebook_diff etc.) in sys_help + sys_tool_describe with a `pack:` tag so grep-driven researchers can find them.
Fix tool_qualitative_codebook_diff output filename to reflect the version numbers in the input (or accept explicit version labels) — silent overwrite is a data-loss bug.

Document a single canonical entry point for natural-language routing (`tool_route` vs `tool_semantic_route` vs `tool_quick_route` vs `sys_semantic_tool_search`). The researcher cannot tell when to use which.
Have qualitative_research.design_protocol emit workspace/study_design.yaml so tool_qualitative_select_standard's auto mode has a file to read. Currently auto silently falls to SRQR for everyone.
